text.title: Strategy Analysis — Future State — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Strategy Analysis — Future State must enforce.

rule.statement: Every future-state outcome traces back to a metric in the business-need spec.
rule.rationale: Outcomes that don't trace to need produce future-states that solve the wrong problem.

rule.statement: Every outcome carries a measurable target (number + unit + measurement source).
rule.rationale: Qualitative outcomes ('improve experience') cannot be assessed and breed wishful thinking.

rule.statement: Every outcome is judged reachable from current state within the program horizon; unreachable outcomes are flagged.
rule.rationale: Future-states untethered from reality fail in build phase or produce unwinnable RFPs.

rule.statement: The spec names a single approver (sponsor or steering chair) and an approval-by date.
rule.rationale: Approval drift means the spec rots before build starts.

rule.statement: The spec MUST NOT name specific vendors or products; capabilities and outcomes only.
rule.rationale: Naming vendors pre-empts options analysis and risks RFP bias.
